active_paradigms.idea: Direct geometric construction with hexagonal lattice and explicit radius computation
active_paradigms.description: Construct the packing explicitly without optimization. Place 4 larger circles at corners (touching two edges each), place edge circles along each side, then fill interior with hexagonal lattice positions. For each circle, compute maximum possible radius by checking distances to all edges and neighbors. Use iterative radius expansion: start small, expand each circle greedily in random order until touching something. This exploits the geometric structure directly rather than searching a 78-dimensional space.
active_paradigms.cautions: Must ensure all 26 circles are valid, use tight tolerances (0.9999 factor), verify constraints before returning

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr and hessian approximation for better constraint handling
active_paradigms.description: Switch from COBYLA to trust-constr method which handles nonlinear constraints more robustly. Use 'l-bfgs-b' for Hessian approximation. Structure constraints as: pairwise distances >= r_i + r_j (325 constraints), and 4 boundary constraints per circle. Use tighter tolerances (tol=1e-9). Start from best known configuration rather than random starts. Trust-constr uses interior-point methods that can navigate narrow feasible regions better than COBYLA's simplex method.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: joint position-radius optimization with better constraint handling
active_paradigms.cautions: trust-constr requires constraint Jacobians for efficiency - use sparse structure, may need more iterations

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.shgo (simplicial homology global optimization) for global search with built-in constraint handling
active_paradigms.description: SHGO is a global optimizer that uses simplicial homology to partition the search space and identify local minima. Unlike basin hopping which uses random jumps, SHGO systematically explores the feasible region. Define the objective as negative sum of radii, and constraints as nonlinear constraints. Use sampling methods='sobol' for better coverage. SHGO can find all local minima and return the global one, which is crucial for this multi-modal optimization landscape.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: global optimum search across all local minima
active_paradigms.cautions: SHGO can be slower - use appropriate iteration limits, may need to reduce dimension by fixing some positions

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Optimize all 78 variables (52 position coordinates + 26 radii) simultaneously using SLSQP. Define inequality constraints: boundary constraints (x_i - r_i >= 0, x_i + r_i <= 1) are linear; non-overlap constraints (||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j) are nonlinear. Pack variables as [x1,y1,...,x26,y26,r1,...,r26]. Use current layout as initial guess with multiple random restarts. The optimizer will adjust both positions AND radii to maximize sum.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through joint optimization of all positions and sizes
tried_paradigms.cautions: Non-overlap constraints are non-convex; use 10-20 random restarts. Set ftol=1e-8 for precision. The 325 non-overlap constraints require careful implementation. Provide analytic Jacobians if possible. May need maxiter=1000+ for convergence.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution with constraint penalty function
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate as bound-constrained global optimization. Variables: positions in [0,1], radii in [0,0.5]. Objective: -sum(radii) + penalty_weight * sum(max(0, violation)^2) for all constraints. Use strategy='best1bin', popsize=15, mutation=(0.5,1), recombination=0.7. Differential evolution explores globally and escapes local minima that trap SLSQP. Run with workers=-1 for parallelization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: sum of radii through global stochastic search across the entire solution space
tried_paradigms.cautions: Penalty weight critical - start with 1000, increase if constraints violated. 78 dimensions is high; consider reducing by exploiting symmetry (place circles in symmetric pairs). Set maxiter=2000+ and tol=1e-7. Computationally expensive but finds better global solutions.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi with Lloyd relaxation for geometric position optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Exploit the geometric relationship between circle packing and Voronoi diagrams. Start with initial positions, compute Voronoi tessellation using scipy.spatial.Voronoi. For each cell, compute the maximum inscribed circle radius (distance to nearest Voronoi edge or boundary). Apply Lloyd relaxation: move each center toward its cell centroid weighted by radius potential. Iterate until convergence. Finally, use local radius optimization on the converged positions.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: positions that maximize available space for each circle through geometric structure
tried_paradigms.cautions: Standard Voronoi ignores radii; for true optimality need power diagram (weighted Voronoi). Handle boundary cells (extend to infinity) by clipping to unit square. Lloyd may converge to local optima - try multiple initializations. Combine with final SLSQP refinement on radii only for best results.

tried_paradigms.idea: scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr for joint position-radius optimization
tried_paradigms.description: Optimize all 78 variables (x, y, r for each of 26 circles) jointly using trust-constr method which handles many nonlinear constraints efficiently. Define objective as negative sum of radii. Constraints: (1) 325 pairwise non-overlap constraints: ||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j, (2) 104 boundary constraints: x-r>=0, x+r<=1, y-r>=0, y+r<=1. Bounds: 0<=x,y<=1, r>=0. Use current layout as initial guess. Trust-constr is specifically designed for problems with many constraints and uses a trust-region approach that handles nonlinear constraints better than SLSQP.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of radii by optimizing both positions and radii simultaneously
tried_paradigms.cautions: With 429 constraints, ensure constraint Jacobians are computed efficiently. Set maxiter=1000, gtol=1e-6. May need multiple restarts from different initial layouts. Monitor for constraint violations in final output.

tried_paradigms.description: Use position-only formulation with 52 variables (x, y for each circle). Radii are computed implicitly as r_i = min(wall_distance, min_{j≠i}||c_i - c_j||), guaranteeing valid solutions. Define objective function that computes radii from positions and returns negative sum. Bounds: [(0,1)]×52. Use strategy='best1bin', maxiter=500, popsize=10, tol=1e-7, mutation=(0.5,1), recombination=0.7. This global optimizer can escape local optima and find fundamentally better position configurations than the fixed layout.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Find globally optimal positions that maximize sum of radii through population-based search
tried_paradigms.cautions: Objective is non-smooth (piecewise linear) due to min operations. Use seed=42 for reproducibility. Set polish=True for local refinement. Runtime may be 60-120 seconds for 500 iterations with population ~520.

tried_paradigms.idea: scipy.optimize.basinhopping for global position optimization with local refinement
tried_paradigms.description: Combine global exploration with local minimization using basinhopping. Optimize 52 position variables with implicit radii computation. Use L-BFGS-B as local minimizer with bounds [(0,1)]×52. Parameters: niter=200, T=1.0 (temperature for accepting worse solutions), stepsize=0.1. The algorithm performs random perturbations followed by local minimization, allowing escape from local minima. Use take_step callback with adaptive step size. This hybrid approach can find better layouts than pure local optimization while being faster than differential_evolution.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Global search over position space with local refinement at each step
tried_paradigms.cautions: Tune temperature T based on objective scale (sum_radii ~2-3, so T=0.5-1.0). Use seed for reproducibility. The non-smooth objective may cause L-BFGS-B warnings; consider Nelder-Mead as alternative local minimizer. Track best solution across all iterations.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with COBYLA method for constraint handling
tried_paradigms.description: COBYLA (Constraint Optimization BY Linear Approximation) handles nonlinear constraints differently from trust-constr. It constructs linear approximations of the constraint surface and objective function, then moves along the approximated feasible region boundary. This different mechanism may navigate the complex constraint landscape better. Encode bounds as additional constraints since COBYLA doesn't support bounds directly. Use the same 78-variable formulation with all 429 constraints (325 pairwise + 104 boundary).
tried_paradigms.cautions: COBYLA requires constraints to be functions returning constraint values. Bounds must be added as inequality constraints (x-r>=0 becomes x-r>=0). Use rhobeg=0.1 and rhoend=1e-6 for initial and final trust region sizes. May need more iterations than trust-constr.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.shgo for global optimization with simplicial homology
tried_paradigms.description: SHGO (Simplicial Homology Global Optimization) uses a fundamentally different approach than differential_evolution. It constructs a simplicial complex over the feasible region and uses homology groups to identify basins of attraction, then performs local minimization in each basin. This systematic exploration of the feasible region can find global minima that local methods miss. Use the same constraint formulation with NonlinearConstraint objects.
tried_paradigms.cautions: SHGO can be computationally expensive for high dimensions. Use sampling options like n=128 or n=256 for initial sampling. Specify iters=3 or more for thorough exploration. The constraint handling is built-in but requires proper formulation. May timeout with too many iterations.

tried_paradigms.idea: Direct geometric construction with hexagonal lattice and maximal radius computation
tried_paradigms.description: Instead of optimization, directly construct positions using mathematical formulas based on hexagonal lattice packing. Place 4 corner circles at maximal radii, then place edge circles and interior circles in a hexagonal pattern. Compute each radius as the minimum of: distance to nearest boundary, and half the distance to nearest neighbor. Use scipy.optimize.minimize only for fine-tuning edge circle positions (reduced dimensionality from 78 to ~10-20 parameters for edge adjustments).
tried_paradigms.cautions: The direct construction must produce valid packings. Use hexagonal spacing of approximately 0.2 for interior circles. Edge circles need careful positioning - use golden ratio or optimal edge packing formulas. After construction, verify all constraints and compute maximal radii by solving the linear system of pairwise distance constraints.
